page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with multi-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is rated at a 20 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded compression/decompression, consistently rank in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth and large block write bandwidth scoring in the top 25%. Passmark CPU encryption tests also show strong performance in the top 25%, while database operations, static web serving, and Redis SET operations maintain average middle 50% marks.

The primary tradeoff of the m5ad.24xlarge is its weak single-threaded execution speed, which limits its suitability for single-core bound tasks. However, its high core count, strong memory write bandwidth, and substantial 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage make it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads. The instance is suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, where parallel processing can offset the lower single-core clock speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities; single-threaded operations consistently rank in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth perform in the average to strong ranges. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and static web serving that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local NVMe storage, rather than raw single-core speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge features 96 AMD vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose instance combining high multi-core capacity and fast local NVMe SSD storage for parallelized workloads and database operations.

384 GB of DDR4 memory with strong cached read and large block write bandwidth
3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for multi-threaded databases, static web serving, and parallel compilation
